name: Cross-Account Search
theme: Find any message across all accounts without knowing where it lives.

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search' command exists and runs without error when given mocked AppleScript output representing messages across multiple accounts
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from alice' via CliRunner with mock_osascript returning canned output for messages across 2+ accounts
Verify exit code is 0
Verify output contains message data from the canned results
criteria.threshold: Command succeeds with exit code 0 and search results visible in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search' requires at least one search filter (--from, --subject, --body, --since, or --before); running with no filters produces a usage error
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search' with no filter arguments via CliRunner
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify error message indicates that at least one search criterion is required
criteria.threshold: Non-zero exit code and informative error when no search filter is given

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --from <sender>' filters results to messages matching the sender (case-insensitive substring match)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from alice --json' with mocked data containing messages from alice@example.com and bob@example.com across multiple accounts
Parse JSON output and verify only messages from alice appear
Verify the filter is case-insensitive (e.g. '--from Alice' matches 'alice@example.com')
criteria.threshold: Only messages matching the sender filter appear in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --subject <text>' filters results to messages matching the subject (case-insensitive substring match)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --subject invoice --json' with mocked data containing messages with various subjects
Parse JSON output and verify only messages with 'invoice' in the subject appear
Verify the filter is case-insensitive
criteria.threshold: Only messages matching the subject filter appear in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --body <text>' filters results to messages whose body content contains the given text (case-insensitive substring match)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --body urgent --json' with mocked data where some messages have 'urgent' in their body and some do not
Parse JSON output and verify only messages containing 'urgent' in their body appear
Verify the filter is case-insensitive
criteria.threshold: Only messages whose body matches the content filter appear in output

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --since <date> --before <date>' filters results by date range, and each can be used independently
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --since 2025-01-01 --before 2025-02-01 --json' with mocked data spanning multiple months
Parse JSON output and verify only messages within the date range appear
Verify --since alone works (no upper bound), and --before alone works (no lower bound)
criteria.threshold: Date range filters correctly include/exclude messages by date

criteria.description: Multiple search filters can be combined (e.g., '--from alice --subject invoice --since 2025-01-01') and all are applied conjunctively
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from alice --subject invoice --json' with mocked data containing mixed messages
Parse JSON output and verify only messages matching ALL specified filters appear
criteria.threshold: All specified filters are applied together (AND logic)

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search' without --account searches across ALL accounts, and results include the account name for each message
testSteps: Mock two accounts ('Work' and 'Personal') each with messages
Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --json' without --account
Parse JSON output and verify messages from both accounts appear
Verify each result includes an 'account' field identifying which account it came from
criteria.threshold: Search spans all accounts; each result shows the account name

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --account <name>' scopes the search to a single account
testSteps: Mock two accounts ('Work' and 'Personal') each with messages
Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --account Work --json'
Parse JSON output and verify only messages from the 'Work' account appear
criteria.threshold: Only messages from the specified account appear when --account is used

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --mailbox <name>' scopes the search to a specific mailbox within the targeted account(s)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --mailbox Sent --json' with mocked data
Verify the AppleScript targets the 'Sent' mailbox specifically
Verify results reflect the mailbox scope
criteria.threshold: Search targets the specified mailbox; results reflect the scoped mailbox

criteria.description: Search results include the mailbox name for each message, so the user knows where in their folder structure the message lives
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --json' with mocked data from different mailboxes
Parse JSON output and verify each result includes a 'mailbox' field
criteria.threshold: Each search result includes a 'mailbox' field

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --limit N' caps the number of results returned, with a default of 25
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --limit 2 --json' with mocked data containing 5+ matching messages
Verify JSON output contains at most 2 messages
Verify that without --limit, the default cap is 25 (inspect code or test with >25 messages)
criteria.threshold: --limit caps output; default limit is 25

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search' displays a Rich table with columns for: account, mailbox, date, from, subject, and message ID
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test' (non-JSON mode) with mocked data
Verify the output includes account name, mailbox name, date, from, subject, and message ID per result
Verify the output is formatted as a table (not raw text)
criteria.threshold: Table shows account, mailbox, date, from, subject, and message ID per result

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --json' outputs valid JSON array with objects containing id, account, mailbox, date, from, subject, read, and flagged fields
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --json' via CliRunner with mocked data
Parse stdout as JSON
Verify it is a list of objects
Verify each object has id, account, mailbox, date, from, subject, read (boolean), and flagged (boolean) fields
criteria.threshold: Valid JSON array with all required fields per search result

criteria.description: Search results are sorted by date descending (newest first), consistent with 'messages list'
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test --json' with mocked data containing messages with different dates from different accounts
Parse JSON output and verify dates are in descending order (newest first), interleaved across accounts
criteria.threshold: Results ordered newest-first regardless of which account they came from

criteria.description: Cross-account search uses one osascript call per account (batch fetching), not one per message or one per mailbox
testSteps: Mock two accounts and run 'mailctl messages search --from test'
Inspect mock_osascript.calls to count invocations
Verify there are at most N+1 osascript calls for N accounts (one to get the account list + one per account for searching)
criteria.threshold: At most N+1 osascript calls for N accounts (account list + one search per account)

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search' with no matching results produces an empty table (not an error), and '--json' produces an empty JSON array
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from nonexistent --json' with mocked data containing no matches
Parse JSON output and verify it is an empty array []
Run without --json and verify exit code is 0 (no error)
criteria.threshold: Empty results produce exit code 0 with empty table/JSON array, not an error

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search' produces a clear error when Mail.app is not running
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test' with mock_osascript configured to simulate Mail.app not running
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify error message mentions Mail.app
criteria.threshold: Non-zero exit code and user-friendly error message on stderr

criteria.description: Search command sends data to stdout and errors to stderr, following the convention established in Sprints 2 and 3
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --from test' successfully and verify data on stdout
Run 'mailctl messages search --from test' with an error and verify error message on stderr
criteria.threshold: Stdout for data, stderr for errors, consistent with prior sprints

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages search --help' describes all options including --from, --subject, --body, --since, --before, --account, --mailbox, --limit, and --json
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages search --help' via CliRunner
Verify the help text mentions --from, --subject, --body, --since, --before, --account, --mailbox, --limit, and --json options
Verify the help text includes a clear description of what the command does
criteria.threshold: Help output mentions all search options with descriptions

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages --help' now lists 'search' alongside 'list' and 'show' as available subcommands
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages --help' via CliRunner
Verify the output lists 'search' as an available subcommand
Verify 'list' and 'show' (from Sprint 3) are still listed
criteria.threshold: 'search' visible in messages subcommand help, alongside existing subcommands

criteria.description: Unit tests exist for 'messages search' covering: success with multiple accounts, each filter option (--from, --subject, --body, --since, --before), combined filters, --account scoping, --mailbox scoping, --limit, --json output, empty results, and no-filter error
testSteps: Run pytest for the search test module or messages test module

Verify tests exist covering: multi-account success, --from filter, --subject filter, --body filter, date filters, combined filters, account scoping, mailbox scoping, --limit, --json, empty results, no-filter error
Verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: At least 12 distinct test cases for messages search, all passing

criteria.description: Full test suite (pytest from project root) passes with zero failures, including all Sprint 1, 2, 3, and 4 tests
testSteps: Run 'pytest -v' from the project root
Verify all tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
Verify Sprint 1, 2, and 3 tests still pass (regression check)
criteria.threshold: All tests pass, exit code 0

criteria.description: Search implementation follows the established architecture: AppleScript generation in build_*_script() functions, parsing in parse_*_output() functions, and thin Typer command handlers that delegate to fetch functions and render_output()
testSteps: Inspect the search implementation in src/mailctl/commands/messages.py (or a dedicated search module)
Verify AppleScript generation is in a build_search_script() or similar function
Verify output parsing is in a parse_search_output() or similar function
Verify the command handler uses the shared render_output() and handle_mail_error() from the output module
criteria.threshold: Search follows the build/parse/fetch/render architecture pattern from prior sprints

criteria.description: 'mailctl --version', 'mailctl accounts list', 'mailctl mailboxes list', 'mailctl messages list', and 'mailctl messages show' all still work correctly (Sprint 1, 2, and 3 functionality preserved)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --version' and verify it prints version and exits 0
Run 'mailctl --help' and verify it lists accounts, mailboxes, and messages as command groups
Run existing Sprint 1, 2, and 3 unit tests and verify they still pass
criteria.threshold: All Sprint 1, 2, and 3 functionality still works

outOfScope: Full-text body indexing or search engine (search is done via AppleScript's whose clause or Python post-filtering)
Fuzzy matching or regex-based search — substring match is sufficient
Attachment content search — search covers from, subject, body text, and date only
